ul.li: Mechanics: The thread utilizes a short-term std::mutex or RWLock to ensure pointer stability during the copy.
Performance: A 32KB memcpy takes ~2-5 microseconds on modern L3 cache. Total lock hold time including overhead is typically < 50µs.
Consistency: The snapshot is a "Point-in-Time" view. Any modifications occurring after Step 3 or Step 6 are captured by the Write-Ahead Log (WAL) and replayed during recovery if necessary.

Staleness: If T4 commits during Step 4, the snapshot shows T4 as 'Active'. This is expected; the recovery 'Analysis' phase will see the subsequent 'COMMIT' record in the log and fix the in-memory state.
